sections.paragraph: an unused root, Ch. and Arab. قطب to cut, hence to cut off. (Kindred roots are קָצַב, חָצַב, חָטַב. The biliteral stock קט has the signification of cutting, cutting off, the same as the cognates קץ, חץ; see the roots קָטַל , קָטֹן , קָטַף , Arab. قطّ, قطع and compare at קָצַץ, חָצַץ, גָּזַז, גָּדַד. See also the remarks of Jul. Klaproth, in Merian, De l’Etude Comparative des Langues, p. 216.) Hence-
